description: Baseline run completed but metric parser failed on ANSI-colored vitest summaries

asi.hypothesis: Establish full 1.0 sweep baseline after fixing SurrealDB and harness setup.
asi.error: Suites actually ran, but autoresearch.sh parsed all failures as 999 because Vitest summary lines contain ANSI escape codes between numbers and words.
asi.observed_true_counts: query-presets 0 failed/11 passed; trash 0 failed/97 passed; uploads 4 failed/98 passed; joins 28 failed/46 passed/1 skipped; queues 71 failed/1 passed/2 skipped.
asi.rollback_reason: Invalid metric extraction, not an adapter regression.
asi.next_action_hint: Strip ANSI escape codes before summary parsing, then rerun baseline. Expected valid primary baseline around 103 failures if same results reproduce.

description: Valid full 1.0 objective sweep baseline

asi.hypothesis: Use a broad objective 1.0 blocker metric across green guardrails plus remaining uploads/joins/queues suites.
asi.baseline: blocker_failures=103 = uploads 4 + joins 28 + queues 71; query-presets and trash are green.
asi.next_action_hint: Largest objective win is queues: failures stem from seed duplicate email before most queue tests run. Fixing payload_jobs/deleteMany/drop/reset behavior may unlock ~70 tests quickly. Joins next largest; uploads has 2 env paste-url failures plus 2 stale file collision failures.

description: Populate drizzle schema for resetDB

asi.hypothesis: Giving the adapter a drizzle._.schema would let Payload resetDB delete all tables before queue seeding, eliminating duplicate user failures.
asi.rollback_reason: Worse primary metric: queues went from 71 to 72 failures. The helper then treated the adapter as full Drizzle and createDrizzleSnapshot crashed on missing db.drizzle.query[tableName].fullSchema.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not expose a populated drizzle._.schema unless also implementing enough drizzle.query/select snapshot surface. Better options: implement clearDatabase and avoid 'drizzle' branch, or enhance the existing drizzle shim to support snapshot APIs.

description: Implement fuller drizzle snapshot shim for queue reset

asi.hypothesis: A minimal drizzle query/select/insert shim plus schema would let Payload clearAndSeed reset and snapshot queue fixtures correctly.
asi.rollback_reason: Still worse than baseline. resetDB attempted DELETE FROM posts_rels from the exposed schema, but that table was registered in adapter metadata without a matching DEFINE TABLE, causing reset errors and duplicate seed failures.
asi.next_action_hint: If retrying this path, either define every table exposed in drizzle._.schema (including *_rels/locales) or only expose actually-defined tables. Avoid partial schema entries that resetDB will delete.

description: Add reset/snapshot-compatible drizzle shim for queue fixtures

asi.hypothesis: Expose a minimal but complete drizzle-compatible schema/query/select/insert shim and define all metadata tables so Payload clearAndSeed can reset and snapshot queue fixtures.
asi.result: queues improved from 71 failed / 1 passed to 35 failed / 37 passed; full 1.0 blocker metric improved from 103 to 67.
asi.learned: Queue failures were substantially caused by reset/snapshot incompatibility, not only updateJobs runtime behavior. Defining all metadata tables exposed in the shim avoids DELETE missing-table errors.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue queues: inspect remaining 35 failures. Likely now true job lifecycle/updateJobs/concurrency semantics rather than seed reset.

description: Normalize drizzle snapshot records before restore

asi.hypothesis: Queue login failures after snapshot restore were caused by raw Surreal record IDs in drizzle snapshot rows, so restored users could not be found by Payload string ID.
asi.result: queues improved from 35 failed / 37 passed to 11 failed / 61 passed; full blocker metric improved from 67 to 43.
asi.learned: Snapshot rows exposed through the drizzle shim must be normalized with normalizeDocument before caching/restoring, otherwise IDs become record objects and auth lookups fail after restore.
asi.next_action_hint: Inspect remaining 11 queue failures. They likely concern true queue execution semantics: updateJobs limit/sort, deleteJobOnComplete, retries, cancellation, concurrency/supersedes, or nested task status updates.

description: Try cleaning generated upload files before uploads suite

asi.hypothesis: Cleaning stale generated upload files before the uploads suite would remove filename collision failures and reduce blocker_failures by 2.
asi.rollback_reason: The cleanup pattern was too broad and deleted fixture file animated.webp, causing uploads onInit to fail and uploads_failures=999.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not broad-delete test/uploads files. If addressing uploads noise, only delete known generated static directories or use Payload's seedDB uploadsDir cleanup; first restore fixtures with git checkout in the Payload repo.

description: Treat adapter-populated join fields as non-localized for afterRead

asi.hypothesis: Payload afterRead was stripping or hoisting adapter-populated join pagination objects when the sanitized join field inherited localized=true from its target relationship; marking join fields non-localized should preserve populated join result shapes.
asi.result: joins improved from 28 failed / 46 passed to 25 failed / 49 passed; uploads also improved by one noisy file-collision failure; full blocker metric improved from 43 to 39.
asi.learned: For this adapter, join result objects are already locale-resolved and should not be treated as localized field storage by Payload's afterRead recursion.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue joins: remaining failures still include collection-array sorting/query shape, access/filter/pagination, and top-level where-by-join timeouts. Consider implementing join-aware where prefilter instead of allowing recursive population loops.

description: Add join page metadata and slicing

asi.hypothesis: Implementing page/start slicing and page metadata for join fields would reduce REST/GraphQL join pagination failures.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened from 39 to 40. Joins improved by 1 (25→24 failures), but queues regressed by 1 and uploads noisy failures increased by 1, so under full 1.0 rubric this is not a keep.
asi.next_action_hint: The pagination change is promising for joins and may be worth reapplying later with a queues guard or after stabilizing noisy uploads/queues. It likely fixed one join pagination case but must not be kept alone under current primary metric.

description: Support nested object join options lookup

asi.hypothesis: Some nested join failures may come from Payload passing joins as nested objects instead of dot-path keys, so resolving join options by walking path segments would restore sort/limit/page for group joins.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened from best 39 to 41. Joins did not improve versus best and queues/uploads were noisier in this full sweep.
asi.next_action_hint: Nested join options lookup alone is not sufficient; group join sort failure likely comes from Payload afterRead repopulation or adapter result shape rather than missing joinOptions. Investigate targeted join logs before retrying.

description: Clean ignored upload artifacts before uploads conformance sweep

asi.hypothesis: The remaining non-environment upload failures in the full sweep were caused by ignored/generated upload artifacts from prior runs, so cleaning ignored files in test/uploads before the uploads suite should produce clean-env upload results without deleting tracked fixtures.
asi.result: uploads improved from 3 failed / 99 passed at best to 2 failed / 100 passed; blocker_failures improved from 39 to 38. The two remaining upload failures are the known localhost/nginx paste-url status expectations.
asi.learned: Use git clean -fdX scoped to test/uploads, not broad file deletion. This removes ignored generated artifacts while preserving tracked fixture files such as animated.webp.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with true adapter blockers: joins 25 failures and queues 11 failures. Uploads can be considered clean-env green except for the documented local nginx paste-url issue.

description: Use Surreal atomic append for top-level $push updates

asi.hypothesis: Queue parallel task log loss was caused by read-merge-write races for top-level $push updates, so translating $push into Surreal atomic array append should preserve concurrent log entries.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened from 38 to 60; queues regressed from 11 to 33 failures. Atomic append via SET log += [item] caused updateJob calls to return null / jobs to lose expected log state in queue execution paths.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not use naive Surreal += for $push. If revisiting concurrent log updates, test Surreal array semantics in isolation and ensure UPDATE returns the updated full document. A safer path may be transactional compare/merge or dedicated log sub-records, but avoid changing this without targeted queue tests first.

description: Use lexicographic join sorting to match Payload string sort semantics

asi.hypothesis: Several join sort failures were caused by adapter-side join sorting using numeric string collation, which orders titles like 'test 14' after 'test 9' differently than Payload/DB lexicographic string sort expectations.
asi.result: joins improved from 25 failed / 49 passed to 21 failed / 53 passed; full blocker metric improved from 38 to 35 despite one noisy queue failure regression.
asi.learned: Join field sorting should use plain lexicographic string comparison, not numeric collation. Numeric-aware sorting overfits human-natural ordering and breaks Payload's expected string order for titles with embedded numbers.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue joins. Remaining failures now likely include localized/versioned joins, collection-array explicit sort/filter, access filters, and top-level where-by-join timeouts.

description: Honor join page offsets and pagination metadata

asi.hypothesis: After lexicographic sorting stabilized join order, implementing adapter-side join page offsets and metadata should fix REST join pagination without the earlier noisy regressions.
asi.result: joins improved from 21 failed / 53 passed to 20 failed / 54 passed; full blocker metric improved from 35 to 33.
asi.learned: Join pagination needs start=(page-1)*limit slicing plus hasPrevPage/hasNextPage/page/pagingCounter metadata. This is now a real improvement when combined with correct string sort semantics.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue joins. Remaining failures include localized/versioned joins, collection-array explicit sort/filter, access filtering, and top-level where-by-join timeouts.

description: Apply join where filters during adapter join fetches

asi.hypothesis: Join field `where` config and requested join `where` options were not applied in adapter-side join population, causing filter-specific join tests to return all joined docs.
asi.result: joins improved from 20 failed / 54 passed to 18 failed / 56 passed; full blocker metric improved from 33 to 32 despite one noisy queue regression.
asi.learned: Adapter join fetches need to compile join where filters into the target collection SELECT before grouping by parent. This directly fixes join filtering cases and is not benchmark-specific.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue joins. Remaining likely include localized/versioned joins, collection-array explicit sort/filter, access filtering, and top-level where-by-join timeouts. Consider merging field.where with joinOptions.where instead of choosing one if a remaining failure shows combined filters are wrong.

description: Handle relationTo filters in polymorphic join queries

asi.hypothesis: Polymorphic join where clauses include virtual relationTo predicates that should select which target collections are queried, not be compiled as fields on target documents.
asi.result: joins improved from 18 failed / 56 passed to 15 failed / 59 passed; full blocker metric improved from 32 to 29 despite one noisy queue regression.
asi.learned: For adapter-populated polymorphic joins, relationTo is result metadata. The adapter must consume relationTo filters per target collection and remove matching predicates before compiling the target collection WHERE clause.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue joins. Remaining failures include localized/versioned joins, collection-array explicit sort/filter shape, access filtering, and top-level where-by-join timeouts.

description: Resolve localized join values by request locale

asi.hypothesis: Localized join failures may be caused by flattenJoinValues scanning all locales or defaulting incorrectly instead of using the request locale when resolving localized relationship arrays.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric was unchanged at 29, so no objective improvement. It did not reduce localized join failures in the full 1.0 sweep.
asi.next_action_hint: Localized join failures are likely not simply locale selection in flattenJoinValues. Investigate target document locale flattening / fallback and version-draft join sources before retrying.

description: Reduce client-side virtual join population depth

asi.hypothesis: Top-level where-by-join tests time out because client-side virtual filtering populates relationship/join graphs to depth 5; depth 1 may be sufficient for join field predicates and reduce recursive work.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric was unchanged at 29 despite faster runtime, so per full 1.0 rubric this is not a keep.
asi.next_action_hint: Depth reduction improved duration but not failures. To fix top-level join where tests, implement true join-aware parent prefiltering or improve docMatchesWhere for join pagination objects rather than only reducing population depth.

description: Traverse join pagination docs in getValueAtPath

asi.hypothesis: Top-level where-by-join failures might come from docMatchesWhere/getValueAtPath not traversing join pagination objects like {docs:[...]} for paths such as relatedPosts.title or relatedPosts equals postID.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric unchanged at 29. The added traversal did not reduce join failures, so top-level where-by-join timeouts are likely elsewhere (possibly whereUsesVirtual forcing too much work or join predicates not being converted to target-parent filters).
asi.next_action_hint: Implement a true join-aware parent prefilter: detect where roots that are join fields, query target collections using the suffix predicate plus join.on relationship back to parent, then filter parent docs by matching IDs. Avoid relying solely on populated docs traversal.

description: Make join-aware client filtering traverse pagination docs at shallow depth

asi.hypothesis: Top-level where-by-join failures require both shallow join population to avoid recursive work and getValueAtPath traversal through join pagination objects; each alone was insufficient, but together should let docMatchesWhere evaluate populated joins quickly.
asi.result: joins improved from 15 failed / 59 passed to 8 failed / 66 passed; full blocker metric improved from 29 to 22 and runtime dropped from ~1298s to ~598s.
asi.learned: Client-side virtual handling for join predicates needs only depth 1 and must treat {docs:[...]} as the traversable value. Deep population caused timeouts; plain traversal without shallow depth did not fix them.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with remaining 8 joins and 12 queues. Inspect remaining joins list; likely localized/versioned joins and collection-array sort/filter remain after top-level where-by-join improvements.

description: Preserve requested join sort separately from default sort

asi.hypothesis: Collection-array join sort failure might be caused by losing whether sort was explicitly requested versus defaulted, so preserving requestedSort could avoid collection fallback ordering overriding explicit sort.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric unchanged at 22; no objective failure reduction.
asi.next_action_hint: Collection-array sort failure remains. Need inspect actual sort path and field.collection array result sorting, not just requested/default sort distinction.

description: Traverse localized wrappers in join target path resolution

asi.hypothesis: Localized array join failures were caused by relationship getValueAtPath not traversing localized wrapper objects like { en: [{ category }] } when resolving join.on paths such as localizedArray.category.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric unchanged at 22. Joins improved by one, but queues improved/noise and joins regressed relative to best 8; no full-rubric improvement.
asi.next_action_hint: The localized wrapper traversal is directionally useful but not sufficient under the current aggregate metric. Reconsider later with another complementary joins fix, or run targeted joins-only validation before reapplying.

description: Populate joins after materializing draft query results

asi.hypothesis: Draft query results are materialized from version.version after the version table read, so join fields on the base collection can be missing unless transformRelationshipReads is applied again to the materialized draft docs.
asi.result: Full blocker metric improved from 22 to 19. Joins improved from 8 failures / 66 passed to 5 failures / 69 passed; green guardrails stayed green and queues were unchanged.
asi.learned: The version-table transform path was not sufficient for draft query joins. Explicitly populating relationships/joins on the materialized base draft documents fixes draft join cases without special-casing tests.
asi.next_action_hint: Remaining joins are likely localized relationship joins, GraphQL group pagination, and multi-collection sort/filter. Inspect latest joins log before selecting next fix.

description: Support nested object join options lookup after draft join fixes

asi.hypothesis: Remaining GraphQL group join pagination might be caused by sanitized joins sometimes being passed as nested objects instead of dot-path keys, so resolving join options by walking path segments should restore nested limit/page/sort.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric unchanged at 19; no objective reduction in remaining blocker failures.
asi.next_action_hint: GraphQL group pagination failure is not fixed by nested join option lookup. Inspect actual joins shape or afterRead/GraphQL layer interaction before retrying. Remaining objective wins may be easier in queues retry/cancel/updateJobs semantics.

description: Pass request locale into join value resolution

asi.hypothesis: Remaining localized join failures may be caused by resolving localized relationship wrapper objects without the request locale, so passing args.locale into join population and flattenJoinValues should filter relationship IDs to the active locale.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric unchanged at 19 and localized join failure count did not improve in the full objective sweep.
asi.next_action_hint: Localized failures are not solved by simply passing locale into flattenJoinValues. Investigate Payload afterRead locale behavior for target docs and localized relationship sort/filter semantics, or switch focus to queue retry/cancel semantics for larger remaining objective wins.

description: Traverse localized wrappers in join path resolution after draft fixes

asi.hypothesis: Remaining localized array join failures may be caused by relationship join path resolution not traversing localized wrapper objects like { en: [{ category }] } for join.on paths.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric unchanged at 19. The run traded one fewer queue failure/noise for one additional joins failure versus best, so no objective improvement under the full 1.0 rubric.
asi.next_action_hint: Localized wrapper traversal remains insufficient and can regress joins. Shift focus to queue retry/cancel/updateJobs semantics or inspect exact remaining join data shape before another localized join attempt.

description: Serialize same-job updates to avoid queue state races

asi.hypothesis: Several queue failures, especially parallel-task and cancellation/retry state transitions, may be caused by concurrent read-merge-write updates to the same payload-jobs document losing fields. Serializing same-job updates in-process should preserve Mongo-like document update semantics for the local queue runner without changing Payload tests.
asi.result: Full blocker metric improved from 19 to 18. Queues improved from 12 failures / 60 passed to 11 failures / 61 passed while joins and green guardrails stayed unchanged.
asi.learned: At least one remaining queue blocker is sensitive to same-job update races; a narrow per-job update lock is safer than the previous Surreal atomic append attempt and preserved existing suites.
asi.next_action_hint: Inspect current queue failure list to see which race-sensitive test turned green. Continue with queue semantics: retry count/backoff/cancel/supersedes remain, and a more complete transactional compare/update or targeted updateJobs claim behavior may reduce more failures.

description: Serialize conditional payload job deletions with job update locks

asi.hypothesis: Supersedes queue failures may be caused by payload_jobs deleteMany racing with runJobs marking a job as processing; serializing conditional job deletions with the same per-job lock and rechecking the where clause should avoid deleting newly-running jobs.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened from best 18 to 19; queues regressed from 11 to 12 failures.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not keep broad locked deleteMany path. The supersedes/running race hypothesis was not validated in the full sweep. Continue with more targeted queue retry/backoff/updateJobs semantics or inspect exact current queue failure diff after the kept per-job update lock.

description: Default localized join flattening to default locale

asi.hypothesis: Localized join failures may occur because default-locale requests pass no explicit locale into adapter join population, causing flattenJoinValues to scan all locale wrappers and include es-only relationships in en results. Defaulting join flattening to Payload's defaultLocale should filter localized relationship IDs unless a request locale overrides it.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric unchanged at 18; no objective failure reduction over the current best.
asi.next_action_hint: Default-locale flattening alone is not enough. Remaining localized failures likely involve target doc locale read transforms or localized join.on path resolution. Consider queue retry/backoff semantics next because the joins attempts are plateauing.

name: Payload SurrealDB full 1.0 readiness and modularity

description: Baseline for expanded full 1.0 readiness objective with modularity metrics

asi.hypothesis: Broaden autoresearch to the full ROADMAP-1.0 suite set plus plugin readiness and code modularity tie-breaker metrics, then establish a fresh baseline before continuing optimization.
asi.baseline: Expanded blocker_failures=1045. Most of the new total comes from plugin_nested_docs_failures=999, which indicates suite execution or summary parsing/setup failure rather than necessarily 999 assertions; plugin_search has 17 failures, database 4, collections_graphql 1, relationships 6, joins 5, uploads 2, queues 11.
asi.result: Expanded objective and modularity extraction were committed as the new baseline. Green core suites mostly remained green, but the broader objective reveals additional 1.0 blockers outside the previous active sweep.
asi.next_action_hint: First inspect /tmp/payload-surrealdb-plugin_nested_docs-autoresearch.log.clean to determine why plugin_nested_docs parsed as 999. Fix harness/setup if it is an environment/parser issue; otherwise address true plugin nested docs blockers. Then tackle plugin_search failures and newly exposed database/relationships regressions.

description: Count setup-time failed suites instead of crash sentinel

asi.hypothesis: The expanded baseline was inflated because plugin-nested-docs times out in beforeAll and reports a failed suite, not 999 individual test failures. Counting Vitest's Failed Suites total when no per-test failure count is available makes blocker_failures reflect real objective failures while still penalizing setup blockers.
asi.result: blocker_failures dropped from 1045 to 48 by measuring plugin_nested_docs_failures as 1 failed suite instead of the crash sentinel 999. This is a metric correctness fix, not a product behavior fix. Other suite counts are broadly unchanged, with queues showing one noisy extra failure in this run.
asi.learned: plugin-nested-docs is a true setup-time blocker: beforeAll times out during seeding and all 11 tests are skipped. It should be investigated next as a functional/plugin compatibility issue, not as a parser crash.
asi.next_action_hint: Inspect plugin-nested-docs seeding and nestedDocs hooks for recursive breadcrumb/update behavior causing init timeout. Next largest true blocker is plugin_search with 17 failures, followed by queues, relationships, joins, database, uploads, and collections_graphql.

description: Include transaction snapshots in findOne reads

asi.hypothesis: Plugin-search failures come from afterChange hooks calling payload.findByID for a document created in the same transaction. The adapter's find path already merges transaction snapshots, but findOne/findByID did not, causing NotFound before commit.
asi.result: blocker_failures improved from 48 to 37. plugin_search improved from 17 failures / 3 passed to 6 failures / 14 passed. Core green suites stayed green; queues remained noisy at 12 failures.
asi.learned: Read-your-writes support was incomplete: findOne must merge queued transaction docs and re-evaluate the where clause against the merged set, not just direct Surreal SELECT results.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue plugin_search: inspect remaining 6 failures. They likely involve reindex/delete/localized edge cases after the basic afterChange findByID path was fixed. Also investigate plugin-nested-docs beforeAll timeout separately.
